Senior Project Coordinator - Buildings
description:
**Build What Matters**


At Graham, one of North America’s largest general contractors, we don’t just construct buildings, we create spaces that shape lives and strengthen communities. As part of our Buildings division, you will contribute to meaningful projects across the region, from hospitals and schools to entertainment venues and multi\-family residences.


Here, your work has purpose. Your safety and growth are supported. And your impact is lasting.

**Join a team that builds with commitment, reliability, and integrity. Apply today and help us build what matters.**

 **Your Impact Starts Here**


Graham is actively recruiting for a Senior Project Coordinator supporting the Buildings Division. Reporting to the Project Manager, the Senior Project Coordinator plays a key role in supporting the coordination, scheduling, quantity surveying, reporting, and quality control functions during the pre\-construction, construction and close out phases of medium to large scale projects.

 **Your Role in Building What Matters – From Office to Site**

* **Support project controls:** Reviewing and updating schedules, budgets, cost tracking, and monitoring labour, materials, and equipment proper allocation
* **Construction Monitoring:**Compare actual construction progress with the schedule, identify critical paths, and stay updated on manpower and material deliveries.
* **Costs Management:**Keep track of daily, weekly, and monthly labour, equipment, material, and subtrade costs and regularly review cost accruals with the Project Manager
* **Prepare and****Manage project documentation** including meeting minutes, action items, drawings, revisions, site instructions, and change orders
* **Oversee change management** by identifying change events, submitting notices, tracking scope changes, and briefing the Project Manager
* **Lead safety and quality processes**by maintaining safety records, supporting the Project Specific Safety Plan (PSSP), monitoring HSE KPIs, and ensuring Quality Plan (PSQP) implementation
* **Provide project reporting and coordination**through progress updates, client satisfaction surveys, and review of field surveillance reports (including initiating NCRs)
 **The Right Tools for the Job**

* Education: Technical Diploma/Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field; or relevant trade certification
* Skills Required: Strong proficiency in MS Office, project management software, and scheduling tools. Ability to manage documentation, track cost and schedule updates, and support safety and quality processes.
* Years of Experience: 3\+ years of construction experience
  

\#LI\-OFFICE \#MANITOBA \#PC \#SENIOR

**Lay the Foundation for Your Next Career Move. Apply Today.**


At Graham, we’re building more than structures, we’re building futures. Join a team that delivers excellence from the ground up and makes a lasting impact across Canada.


We’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and are committed to an inclusive, accessible workplace. Accommodations are available throughout the hiring process when requested. Please contact hiring@jardeg.com for accommodation requests.


Unsolicited resumes or candidate profiles will not be accepted and will become the property of Jardeg/Graham.


As Graham’s trusted recruitment partner, Jardeg supports the hiring of skilled professionals across Buildings, Industrial, Infrastructure, Water, Development, and Services projects nationwide.

Research Technician - Tech 4 (AESES)
description:
Plant Science
Faculty of Agricultural \& Food Sciences  

Position number: 37625
Date posted: March 30, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Research Technician \- Tech 4 (AESES)

New (Budget Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: Yes
  

Work schedule: 35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:30 am to 4:30 pm)
  

Salary: $26\.20 to $37\.62 per hour ($47,684\.00 to $68,468\.40 per annum)
  

Expected Start Date April 27, 2026
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Dr. Linda Schott \- linda.schott@umanitoba.ca (2044748221\)


**Qualifications**
------------------



**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING:**


* B.Sc in agriculture related discipline with three years directly related experience is required.
* Valid driver’s license required.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* Experience conducting agronomic research is required.
* Experience applying pesticides under field research conditions is required.
* Experience using and maintaining field and lab research equipment (hand held and hydraulic soil samplers, weather stations and data loggers, neutron probe, GPS, soil and plant grinders, microscope).
* Experience collecting and organizing agronomic field data and samples, maintaining trial history records, and basic data summary statistics is required.
* Current working knowledge of common agricultural crop production practices used in Western Canada (small grains, oilseeds, legumes, row crops, and forage crops) is required.
* Experience conducting on\-farm, livestock grazing, or interdisciplinary research would be an asset.
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* Effective communication skills (written and verbal) are required.
* Effective verbal and written communication skills required.
* Experimental record keeping, data compilation, summary statistical analysis is required.
* Creating spreadsheets (Excel) and presentations (PowerPoint). Word processing to create summary reports (Word). Summary statistics and graphing data (Excel and SAS) are required. Experience with GIS software would be an asset.
* Ability to coordinate a summer field research program is required.
* Exercise sound judgement when solving field research problems is required.
* Work both independently and as a member of a team is required.
* Multi\-tasking and time management are required.
* Ability to train and supervise summer students are required.
* Physically capable of performing the duties as assigned, which may include mobility (walking, kneeling, bending), moderate to heavy lifting (50 lb seed or fertilizer bags), and working outside in hot/wet/cold conditions during the field season.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.


**OTHER JOB RELATED QUALIFICATIONS:**


* Operating small plot and farm scale equipment (truck and trailer, tractor, seeder, sprayer, swather, combine, forage harvester, mower, tillage equipment).
* Current working knowledge of potato production practices.
* Agronomic research techniques (soil sampling, biomass harvesting, plant stand assessment, crop phenology ratings, scouting for crop pests).
**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


* Execute greenhouse, small plot and field scale research experiments on potatoes and rotational crops under direction of the supervising professor at University of Manitoba Fort Garry campus, on\-farm locations and collaborating partner research stations;
* Identify sites and layout for field experiments
* Communicate with growers and collaborators about research protocols and on\-going research activities
* Prepare experimental materials for planting or deployment in the field
* Establish, maintain, and harvest field research plots. Select equipment and identify optimum timing for field research activities.
* Collect, organize, and process research samples.
* Conduct preliminary statistical analysis of research data.
* Operate, calibrate, and maintain field and lab research equipment.
* Preparation for and participation in field tours of research plots
* Maintain and conduct minor repair work and upgrade field and lab research equipment.
* Execute post\-harvest storage research experiments under direction of the supervising professor at the University of Manitoba Horticulture Storage Facility on the Fort Garry Campus and on\-farm locations;
* Identify sites for storage experiments
* Prepare experimental materials for deployment in storage
* Establish and maintain appropriate storage conditions for on\-going trials
* Coordinate use of University of Manitoba storage facility for collaborators
* Collect, organize, and process research samples
* Conduct preliminary statistical analysis of research data
* Maintain and conduct minor repair work and upgrade storage facility equipment
* Supervision of 1\-4 summer students or other casual help in field, storage, and lab duties.
* Assist graduate students and post\-doctoral fellows in the execution of their project research work. Provide guidance in areas where the candidate is qualified.
* Requisition laboratory and field supplies as well as source experimental materials under direction of the supervising professor.
* Provide training, document training, and keep record of safety training for all staff and students working in the lab group. Develop and review standard operating procedures and safe work procedures for the lab group.
* Execute library research, scan images, and assist in creating presentations.
* Assist with graduate student projects.
* Other duties as assigned.
**Additional information**
--------------------------

  

  

The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).



If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact UM.Accommodation@umanitoba.ca or 204\-474\-7195\. Please note this contact information is for accommodation reasons only.



Application materials, including letters of reference, will be handled in accordance with the protection of privacy provisions of "The Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act" (Manitoba). Please note that curriculum vitae will be provided to participating members of the search process.

title: Research Coordinator - Tech 5 (AESES)
description:
College of Nursing
Rady Faculty of Health Sciences  

Position number: 37638
Date posted: March 30, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Research Coordinator \- Tech 5 (AESES)

New Regular Continuing Full\-Time (Grant Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: Yes
  

Work schedule: 35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:30 am to 4:30 pm)
  

Salary: $28\.46 to $40\.88 per hour ($51,797\.20 to $74,401\.60 per annum)
  

Expected Start Date April 20, 2026
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Mylene David \- mylene.david@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------


**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING:**


* Completion of a masters degree in health\-related field is required.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* Minimum two years of directly related research experience is required.
* Experience in conducting quantitative or qualitative research in social services or health care environments is required.
* Experience with all phases of carrying out a research project; both qualitative and quantitative health research are required.
* Must be familiar with confidentiality and security issues related to research.
* Must have experience with literature searches, and literature retrieval.
* Experience with systematic or scoping reviews is preferred
* Experience with multiple modes of data collection, including individual open\-ended interviews, observational field work, and focus group interviews is required.
* Experience utilizing qualitative and arts\-based data collection and analysis, and artistic knowledge dissemination is required.
* Must have experience using software including: word processing, MS Excel, MS Teams. Experience with EndNote, Covidence, and qualitative research software (e.g., NVivo) is preferred.
* Must have experience with drafting technical reports, brief non\-technical reports for communities, academic publications and presentations, and other written documents as requested.
* Supervision of other research staff an asset.
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**



* Excellent English oral and written communication skills are required.
* Demonstrated interpersonal skills and ability to work respectfully with others is required.
* Ability to work respectfully with community partners, including those experiencing social disadvantage, is required.
* Excellent organizational skills is required.
* Must be able to work within time and budget constraints.
* Must be self\-motivated and be able to work independently.
* Ability to work collaboratively as part of a multidisciplinary team is required.
* Must have access to a vehicle and/or ability to travel locally (e.g., for data collection in healthcare settings.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.
  




**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


* Coordinate day\-to\-day research activities for Drs. Horrill and Udod programs of research (spanning multiple projects) and ensure smooth and efficient operations.
* Monitor and provide regular updates on the progress of research activities to the PI and research team members.
* Assist in the training and orientation of research staff across the PI’s program of research.
* Assigns and directs the work of research support staff, ensuring deadlines are met and research protocol and regulatory requirements at the University of Manitoba, funding agencies, and other organizations where research is conducted are followed.
* Manages ethical approvals, including: preparing documents for ethical review, assisting with obtaining initial ethical approvals, managing requests for ethics amendments and yearly renewals.
* Responsible for recruitment of participants, booking appointments with participants, and obtaining consent from them.
* Responsible for data collection (includes, but not limited to, conducting open\-ended individual interviews, focus group interviews, participant observation, administering questionnaires, and collecting additional information from selected records, such as patient charts).
* Develops and maintains records of research activities (e.g., data management plan).
* Preparing periodic and ad hoc reports as required by investigators, administrators, and funding agencies.
* Contributes and completes, as needed, to research analysis, including interpretation and preparation of research reports, publications, and presentations, as well as assisting with writing of grant proposals; may include literature searching, using a reference management system (e.g. EndNote, ZOTERO), and reviewing.
* Organizes and participates in research team meetings.
* Meets regularly with PI to discuss study progress, issues and status, and quality of data collection.
* Prepares and processes all forms and accounts related to the grant, including monitoring of expenditures.
+ Maintains data files in accordance with ethical guidelines.
+ Coordinates activities for Advisory Boards and Community Advisory Boards (e.g., schedules meetings, manages honoraria payments, provides updates to the CAB, liaises between the CAB and the PI).

* Perform other related duties to the research projects as assigned
**Additional information**
--------------------------

  

  

The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).



If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact UM.Accommodation@umanitoba.ca or 204\-474\-7195\. Please note this contact information is for accommodation reasons only.



Application materials, including letters of reference, will be handled in accordance with the protection of privacy provisions of "The Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act" (Manitoba). Please note that curriculum vitae will be provided to participating members of the search process.

**EXPERIENCE:**



* Must have experience working with people experiencing problematic substance use and experiencing complex and enduring challenges related to the social determinants of health.
* Experience with all phases of carrying out a research project; both qualitative and quantitative health research is required.
* Experience with data collection, individual interviewer\-led surveys, open\-ended interviews, writing field notes, participant observation, focus group interviews, and photovoice is required.
* Experience in conducting quantitative or qualitative research in social services or health care environments is required.
* Experience using computer software related to word processing and data collection is required.
* Must be familiar with confidentiality and security issues related to research essential.
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**



* Excellent people skills and experience working with people experiencing problematic substance use and other structural vulnerabilities is required.
* Excellent English oral and written communication skills are required.
* Demonstrated interpersonal skills and ability to work well with others is required.
* Good organizational skills is reuqired.
* Must be able to work within time and budget constraints.
* Must be self\-motivated and be able to work independently.
* Ability to work collaboratively as part of a multidisciplinary team is required.
* Ability and willingness to learn new processes and techniques required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.
**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


* Ensure smooth and efficient day\-to\-day operation of the project, mainly at Main Street Project:
* Collecting data using a combination of instruments, interviewer\-led surveys, and qualitative interviews
* Locating patients that have been absent from the program and checking in, accompanied by a peer
* Interviewing program participants
* Collaborating with other program staff
* Providing updates and progress reports to members of the research team (including the other part time RA) and stakeholders.

* Monitor the progress of research activities, maintain records of research activities, prepare periodic and ad hoc reports as required by investigators, administrators and funding agencies.

title: Aquatic Invasive Species Monitoring and Response Technician
Manager, Business Intelligence
description:
**Date:** Mar 30, 2026


**Location:** Winnipeg, MB (Hybrid), CA North York \- Toronto, ON (Hybr, CA Kitchener, ON (Hybrid), CA Vancouver, BC (Hybrid), CA Moncton, NB (Hybrid), CA Dartmouth, NS (Hybrid), CA Calgary, AB (Hybrid), CA Edmonton, AB (Hybrid), CA
**Company:** Wawanesa Insurance  



**Job ID:** 9934  

**Employment Type:** Existing Role  

  

**Working Business Language**: English  

**Salary:** At Wawanesa, salary is only one component of a holistic, comprehensive and competitive offering that we provide to our employees. In addition to salary, full\-time and part\-time permanent employees are eligible for an annual bonus plan, leave of absence top\-up programs and provided with generous vacation time, personal days, premium free benefits and pension plan.  


The salary offered for this role is determined with consideration to various factors, including but not limited to: your work location, local labour market conditions, external market salary data, internal pay equity and the knowledge, skills, experience and anticipated proficiency in the role. The salary offered is estimated to be within the following range: $150,000 \- $190,000\. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are still encouraged to apply.  


**About Us**  

At Wawanesa, we offer a hybrid work environment that offers flexibility to our employees in balancing in\-office (2 days per week OR 15 hours per week in a Wawanesa office) and remote work. You may work from any of the following locations: Winnipeg, MB; Vancouver, BC; Calgary, AB; Edmonton, AB; Toronto (North York), ON; Kitchener, ON; Moncton, NB; Dartmouth; NS.  


The Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company (“Wawanesa Mutual”), founded in 1896, is one of Canada’s largest mutual insurers, with over $3\.5 billion in annual revenue and assets of $10 billion (CAD). Wawanesa Mutual, with its National Headquarters in Winnipeg, is the parent company of Wawanesa Life, which provides life insurance products and services throughout Canada, and Western Financial Group, which distributes personal and business insurance across Canada. Wawanesa proudly serves more than 1\.7 million members in Canada, and we are home to more than 3,300 employees distributed across the Canadian regions and communities where we operate. We give back to organizations that strengthen communities, donating more than $3\.5 million annually to charitable organizations, including over $2 million annually in support of people on the front lines of climate change. We are also proud to be recognized as one of Manitoba’s Top Employers. To learn more visit wawanesa.com.  

We are currently looking for dedicated, driven, and enthusiastic individuals who thrive in an environment that welcomes change and are looking for an opportunity for diverse experience and advancement on a growing team. **Job Overview**

As Manager of Business Intelligence, this leadership role is important to embedding a culture of data\-driven decision\-making across the organization. This role is impactful in ensuring that decisions, are underpinned by deep, actionable insights drawn from reliable data. By collaborating closely with organizational leaders, the Manager leads the successful delivery, maintenance, support, and evolution of Wawanesa’s business intelligence and insights architecture, technology, and processes.  


**Job Responsibilities**
* Actively participate in aligning business intelligence operations with enterprise strategy and objectives.
* Manage the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the enterprise business intelligence strategy; KPI and Information framework.
* Work closely with company leaders to understand and manage data and reporting goals. Provide advice on the latest trends and best practices in business intelligence.
* Support a robust business intelligence system and environment to enhance user experience and enable advanced capabilities for analysis, analytics, reporting, and data engineering.
* Generate data driven insights incorporating leading and lagging indicators.
* Manage alignment with architectural, data governance, and enterprise methodologies.
* Maintain consistent delivery processes, focusing on stakeholder satisfaction and tangible benefits.
* Manage effective delivery and communication mechanisms to foster heightened levels of interaction and responsiveness.
* Manage resource needs and diligently monitor both internal and external resources, actively managing the performance of the team within the department.
* Oversee and coach a team to ensure work environment is respectful, challenging and rewarding and employees are maximized to their full potential as we build best\-in\-class data assets across key data domains.
* Attract and recruit top industry talent.
* Perform other duties as assigned.
**Qualifications**
* Minimum of 5 years in data, analytics, technology, or a related field with a minimum of 3 years in a leadership role.
* Post\-secondary degree, preferably in computer science, actuarial science/mathematics, accounting/finance, or related field with a preference for a combination of technical and business study.
* Strong business acumen with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. Capable of recognizing critical issues and making sound decisions in complex, high\-pressure situations.
* Strong interpersonal skills, proficient with verbal and written communication, coupled with the skill to articulate complex information clearly and concisely to audiences of varying technical expertise.
* Excellent ability to collaborate and communicate effectively with a variety of internal stakeholders.
* Highly skilled in creating data\-driven business intelligence solutions for complex and large\-scale technology projects.
* Adept at addressing business challenges through precise identification and the development of compelling business cases.
* Robust understanding of development lifecycles, and working knowledge spanning enterprise, business, application, data, and technology architectures.
* Proficiency in project management methodologies, particularly Agile, with the ability to manage and prioritize a busy workload and multiple projects.
* Ability to inspire and guide teams through change, adapting quickly to new directions.
* Strong leadership, motivation, and decision\-making abilities. Boost team morale through encouragement, coaching, and clear guidance to help them achieve goals.
* Familiarity with modern Data Warehousing, Business Intelligence, Reporting, Data Governance and Stewardship, and Advanced Analytics is considered an asset.
* Experience in the insurance/finance industry, knowledge of ITIL/SAFe, is considered an asset.
  

**Diversity Equity, Inclusion \& Belonging**  

  

At Wawanesa, we are committed to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) and believe that our strength lies in the diversity of our people – this is supported by having a representative workforce.  


We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, including racialized persons, women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, members of the 2SLGBTQIA\+ community, gender\-diverse and neurodiverse individuals, and anyone who can contribute to the further diversification of thought and ideas.  


We aim to ensure our recruitment process is accessible to all candidates. If you require accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please reach out in confidence to jobs@wawanesa.com.  


All Wawanesa job applicants are subject to Wawanesa's Privacy Policy.  


Please note that the recruitment process for this position may involve the use of AI tools to screen, assess, or select applicants. All final decisions are taken or reviewed by human recruiters and human hiring leaders in compliance with all applicable legislation.

title: Business Intelligence Analyst
description:
**About Us**
------------



At CAA Club Group, we are passionate about keeping our Members safe \- whether they are on the road, at home, or travelling abroad. As a trusted Member\-based organization, we provide products and services that are essential to Members’ safety and peace of mind. With over 2\.6 million Members across Ontario and Manitoba, we advocate on issues such road safety and infrastructure, the environment, seniors’ mobility, and consumer protection. Associates share in each other’s successes, drive change and support each other while cultivating a great working environment to deliver on what is best for our Members. CAA SCO Systems \& Services Inc is a member of the CAA Club Group of Companies.

**Who we are**
--------------



Are you ready to join an award\-winning, purpose\-driven culture? Welcome to the CAA Club Group of Companies (CCG), where purpose leads to passion!  

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience. We offer:

* Work\-life harmony with access to an award\-winning holistic wellness program,
* Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,
* Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,
* Pay\-for\-performance and best\-in\-class recognition programs, and
* Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.

  

Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

**Position Details**
--------------------


**What You Will Do:**

* Create comprehensive and automated BI \& Reporting solutions, while focusing on the front\-end interface in Power BI.
* Participate in the stakeholder requirement workshops/meetings and ensure business requirements are adequately captured in SOW.
* In collaboration with BI Developer, support development of data solutions, including data models, data lifecycle management, data access and reporting elements.
* Analyze complex data sets from various database servers and systems to identify insights and trends shaping in each LOB, functional area, and CCG enterprise.
* Translate analysis into self\-serve Power BI dashboards, including visuals, slicers, and measures with a focus on storytelling and user experience.
* In collaboration with BI Developer, create mockup reports for proof\-of\-concept reviews.
* Support lines of business in a variety of analytical needs including development of robust analytical plans, statistical analysis, key performance indicators, benchmarking, data visualizations, routine, and ad hoc reporting.
* Utilize machine learning techniques to proactively identify opportunities for transforming descriptive to predictive analytics.
* Provide analytics support on key projects/initiatives aligned to business or organizational strategies.
* Create technical documents for all newly built data/reporting solutions.
* Serve as a subject matter expert across Finance and assist users with reporting and analysis activities and tools, including ad hoc reporting.
* Support end\-user training, testing, documentation, and roll\-out of reports.
* Maintain knowledge of business processes and application design and understand cross\-functional business relationships and their use of information.
* Work in conjunction with other internal teams on collaborative projects, including data analytics initiatives.
* Keep current on emerging tools and techniques and grow knowledge of the business.

 **Who You Are:**

* Undergraduate degree or post\-secondary education in quantitative field (Computer Science, Finance, Mathematics, Analytics, Economics, etc.).
* Microsoft Power BI or appropriate Analytics certification is desirable.
* 3\+ years of experience developing Power BI dashboards ensuring security, architecture, governance, and performance meets outlined objectives.
* 3\+ years of experience working with databases and warehouses, with hands\-on expertise in SQL\-Server (SSIS, SSRS, SSAS tabular models).
* 3\+ years of experience in analytical languages, including DAX, M, BEx, Power Query, etc.
* Familiarity with ML concepts, frameworks, and libraries along with the ability to execute these in Python/R is considered a strong asset.
* Experience working with SAP BI/BW/BPC is considered a strong plus.
* Attention to detail with high aptitude for problem\-solving.
* Excellent communication skills, proven ability to discuss BI solutions at the appropriate level of detail with all levels of the organization.
* Ability to adjust to changing priorities and participation in multiple projects.
* Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office products (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Access).


Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

**Our Commitment**
------------------



We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with all applicable Provincial Human Rights and Accessibility Legislation. CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

title: Program Coordinator
description:
**Who we are:** **Pulford Community Living Services** is a not\-for\-profit organization that provides community\-based residential support to person with intellectual disabilities. At Pulford, we foster an environment that encourages personal growth and independence, empowering each individual to live a full life. Guided by our core values of Dignity, Safety, Respect, and Integrity, we are committed to creating an equal and inclusive world where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. Join us in our mission to bring communities together, promoting and empowering individuals to lead fulfilling lives.

**Position Summary:**


The Program Coordinator supports individuals we serve in residential/day service settings. They must be a passionate leader who can successfully assist in directing supervisors and support staff to provide high quality services to individuals we support. In addition to ensuring the overall health and safety of the people we support, the Program Coordinator also ensures the effective and efficient use of available resources and will follow through with all job duties consistent with the agency mission, vision, and values.

**Responsibilities**

**Operations \& Administration**

* Oversee scheduling, staffing coverage, and on\-call coordination
* Ensure compliance with licensing, safety, and regulatory requirements
* Maintain accurate documentation (reports, incidents, case files)
* Develop and adjust service strategies based on individual progress
* Monitor program effectiveness through consistent communication with stakeholders
* Report concerns, risks, and required changes to senior leadership
* Direct supervisors and support staff in delivering structured, person\-centered services
* Translate individual plans into daily service execution and measurable outcomes
* Identify service gaps and implement corrective actions
**Staff Leadership**

* Conduct performance reviews and address performance or conduct issues
* Provide ongoing training and real\-time support to frontline teams
* Assist in the recruitment, hiring, training, supervision, coordination and performance of Direct Support staff and Managers
**Financial \& Resource Management**

* Monitor program\-related expenses and individual financial support
* Approve time sheets, payroll inputs, and related administrative processes
* Supervise completion of social allowance annual reviews and income tax
**Stakeholder Coordination**

* Liaise with families, community partners, management, and external agencies
* Maintain consistent communication across internal teams and departments
**Requirements**

**Experience**

* Minimum of 3 years' experience in a leadership role managing staff is required
* Experience handling staff performance, conflict, and service delivery issues
* Background in community services, healthcare, or residential support is an asset
**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree in a related field is preferred
* Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered
**Qualifications and Skills**

* Clear decision\-making in fast\-moving environments
* Strong communication (written and verbal)
* Ability to manage competing priorities without losing structure
* Practical problem\-solving and sound judgment
**Additional Requirements**

* Valid Class 5F Manitoba driver’s license
* Access to a reliable vehicle
* Availability for evenings, weekends, and on\-call rotation
**Why join us?**

* Comprehensive on the job training
* Continuous training for skill development
* Cell phone allowance
* Perks programs
* Goodlife Corporate memberships
* Flexibility
* Great work culture
* Referral Program
* Health and Dental benefits
* Pension Plan
**Additional Details:**

**Status:** Full\-time, Permanent**Work Hours:** 80 Hours Bi\-Weekly – days, evenings and weekends
 (on\-call and drop\-in requirements)  

Closing Date: Until Filled


Salary: DOQ


Work Location: Winnipeg, MB

**Interested?**

**Please visit our website at** **https://pulford.ca/employment** **and click the Apply Now button.**

*We thank all applicants for showing interest in joining Pulford; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.*

title: Residence Life Coordinator - Office Assistant 4 (AESES)
description:
Student Residences
Office of the CFO \- Ancillary Services  

Position number: 20663
Date posted: March 30, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Residence Life Coordinator \- Office Assistant 4 (AESES)

Existing Project Full\-Time (Budget Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: No
  

Work schedule: 35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:30 am to 4:30 pm)
  

Salary: $24\.69 to $33\.63 per hour ($44,935\.80 to $61,206\.60 per annum)
  

Appointment Dates May 2, 2026 to May 2, 2029
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Carolyn Kerr \- Carolyn.Kerr@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------



**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING:**
* Completion of a formal training program in Student Development, Education, Psychology, Sociology, or a related discipline from a recognized institute is required, with knowledge and understanding of student development theories.
* Certified in first aid and CPR; non\-violent crisis intervention is required.
* Bachelors Degree in Student Development, Education, Psychology, Sociology, or a related discipline preferred with knowledge and understanding of student development theories.

  


**EXPERIENCE:**
* A minimum of three years of experience in increasingly responsible positions in a residence environment is required.
* Experience with program planning, training, facilitation, and the implementation of measuring and evaluation tools is required.
* Supervisory and/or management experience is required.
* An acceptable equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.


**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills are required.
* Excellent interpersonal and conflict resolution skills are required.
* Philosophy consistent with a team environment is required.
* Demonstrated proficiency at the intermediate level Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc) is required.
* Certified in first aid and CPR is required.
* Non\-violent crisis intervention is required.
* Ability to organize, schedule, supervise, and assess the work of others is required.
* Ability to motivate staff in a positive manner is required.
* Ability to relate well to students, parents, staff, and general public is required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality is required.


**OTHER JOB RELATED QUALIFICATIONS:**


* Familiarity with University policies and procedures preferred.
* Must have a clean criminal record and child abuse check


* This is a live\-in position. Must be available on call 24\-hours/day (evenings, weekends, and/or holidays) on a rotational basis.


* This position has vacation restrictions in August and September



**Key responsibilities**
------------------------


* Overseen by the Assistant Director, Residence Life hires, trains, and supervises all student staff including Senior Resident Advisors and Resident Advisors, ensuring that appropriate developmental programs are offered.
* Meets weekly with the Assistant Director, Residence Life, Senior Resident Advisors, and Resident Advisors.
* Coordinates the annual training and orientation program for Residence Life staff and new residents, ensuring high quality and relevant sessions.
* Develops, implements, and oversees developmental, educational, and recreational programs for resident students ensuring they are of high quality and broad scope.
* Arranges and conducts annual orientation programs for new residents, as well as parents/family members of residents.
* Oversees the residence room assignment process.
* Initiates appropriate action with respect to disciplinary matters (i.e., noise violations, vandalism, disruptive students, etc.)
* Works with members of the residence life staff and residence security staff to ensure the provision of a safe and secure living environment.
* Ensures the enforcement of community standards through policy interpretation and implementation.
* Consults with the Assistant Director, Residence Life in cases of significant disciplinary issues.
* Participates as part of a weekly on\-call rotation throughout the year to manage and triage urgent, emergency and crisis situations that may arise in residence on evenings and weekends.
* Serves as an advisor to each residences student government in matters related to impact of actions, student advocacy, policy matters, and organizational issues.
* Meets with individual student residents, as necessary, to address issues of distress and/or personal concerns. Refers students to seek out appropriate resources, and facilitates action to address such concerns and issues.
* Assists with the management of Living Learning communities.
* Assists with residence life staffing, budgets, financial sustainability, monthly department reports, and overall strategic planning for the residences.
* Assists with policy and procedure development.
* Acts as a liaison with Residence Security and Campus Security Services to ensure policies and procedures are consistent with University standards.
* Communicates on a regular basis with the Facilities Coordinator and Custodial Manager to address issues related to caretaking and overall building maintenance.
* Develops and conducts campus\-wide developmental and educational programs for the University community.
* Participates in presentations promoting residence life such as Info Days, Evening of Excellence, Emerging Leaders Dinner, and Parents Program.
* Other duties as assigned.


**Additional information**
--------------------------

  



The University of Manitoba is committed to the principles of equity, diversity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring, promotion and tenure (where applicable) for systemically marginalized groups who have been excluded from full participation at the University and the larger community including Indigenous Peoples, women, racialized persons, persons with disabilities and those who identify as 2SLGBTQIA\+ (Two Spirit, lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, questioning, intersex, asexual and other diverse sexual identities).



If you require accommodation supports during the recruitment process, please contact UM.Accommodation@umanitoba.ca or 204\-474\-7195\. Please note this contact information is for accommodation reasons only.



Application materials, including letters of reference, will be handled in accordance with the protection of privacy provisions of "The Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act" (Manitoba). Please note that curriculum vitae will be provided to participating members of the search process.

title: Project Manager
description:
**Job description:**

Wintec Building Services is one of the fastest\-growing heavy civil contractors in Manitoba, and we're expanding our project management group to suit increasing project loads. Based inside the perimeter on Fermor Avenue in Navin, MB, Wintec has established itself as a one\-stop shop, with in\-house capacity for excavation, earth and base works, sewer and water construction, and concrete and asphalt pavements. Our strongly motivated management group is driving for further growth, rapidly creating new opportunities as our team grows.

As a successful applicant for the position, you will join our Projects group to:

· oversee a wide variety of heavy civil projects through all stages of the project process, including estimating, project scheduling, engaging subcontractors and suppliers, coordinating Wintec's own forces, construction scheduling and crew management, overseeing project progress, project invoicing, and post\-construction and warranty inspections.

* prepare and submit pre\-construction and ongoing project submittals
* interact directly with clients, management, and on\-site superintendents and foremen to identify and resolve issues as they arise.
* work with other coordinators and managers to allocate crews and resources between various ongoing projects.
* regularly inspect work sites for which you are responsible, ensuring they meet the expectations and/or contractual requirements of our clients and Wintec's own standards of work.
* support crews with interpretation of contract documents and requirements, obtaining clarification from clients or senior management to confirm the same where required.
* provide regular updates on project status to the project team.

You will be required to have:

· a degree or diploma in engineering, civil technology or construction management, or similar programs

* a minimum of 5 years experience in a heavy civil construction environment, with an emphasis on project and/or construction management in road works or sewer and water works in local conditions
* direct experience with City of Winnipeg and Manitoba Infrastructure contract processes and specifications
* experience estimating and scheduling construction contracts
* the ability to work independently
* strong communication and interpersonal skills
* a valid driver's license

Only selected candidates will be contacted.

Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent

Pay: $60,000\.00\-$115,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Casual dress
* Company car
* Dental care
* Disability insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Extended health care
* Life insurance
* On\-site parking
* Vision care

Work Location: In person

title: Project Manager
description:
**Job Description:**

GME Construction is looking for a skilled individual to join as team as our Project Manager / Estimator. They will be responsible for bidding on construction projects, the planning process and scheduling. They will also control the execution of the project to ensure they stay on schedule and within budget.

**Requirements** 

* Creating plans, managing timelines, controlling budgets and ensuring delivery
* Managing subcontractors and laborers
* Sourcing and supplying necessary materials and equipment
* Serving as the point of communication between the architects, property owners, engineers and the trades
* Analyzes plans and blueprints and create a budget estimate for equipment, labor, materials or industrial projects
* Oversee and manage the lifecycle of a project from start to finish
* Plan, Organize, direct, control and evaluate daily operations
* Ability to negotiate with subcontractors
* Ability to present detailed proposals to clients and stakeholders

**Qualifications**

* Deep understanding of construction processes, codes, and industry pricing.
* Excellent skill in communication, negotiation and leadership
* Bachelor’s degree in construction management, Engineering, Building science, architectural technology or anything in the related field
* Knowledge in blueprint reading, building codes, project scheduling, construction math and budgeting
* Experience in residential and commercial would be an asset
* Experience in the field with several years as a qualified tradesperson
* Certifications such as CEC, CPE, GSC. PMP, CCM would be an asset

Job Type: Full\-time

Benefits:

* Casual dress
* Company events
* Extended health care
* On\-site parking
* Paid time off
* Vision care

Education:

* Bachelor's Degree (preferred)

Licence/Certification:

* Class 5 Licence (required)

Location:

* Winnipeg, MB (required)

title: PROJECT ENGINEER
description:
**Project Engineer**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



The Project Engineer leads the delivery of assigned telecommunications capital projects and programs, ensuring they meet strategic  

objectives. Working under the guidance of the Project Team Lead, the role drives project planning, coordination, and execution from  

concept to completion.


***Responsibilities:***


* Responsible for effectively and efficiently managing Telecommunication Department projects.
* Responsible for budgets, schedules and regular reporting through all stages of assigned programs and projects.
* Responsible for multidisciplinary coordination and ensuring the final product meets technical requirements. Monitor any



changes to the scope of work; notify and obtain authorization from project stakeholders to any changes.  

* Prepare project reports as needed, such as; variance explanations, outlook forecasts, year\-end forecasts, project reports and


summaries, memos, and lessons learned including issues arising from environmental, safety, licensing, design, procurement,  

construction, and commissioning activities.


* Work with end users to ensure deliverables of the program/project meet their immediate needs.
* Frequently collaborates with cross functional teams to manage programs and projects.
* Provide project management expertise to support project participants regarding interdisciplinary engineering, procurement,



financial, safety, environmental, and resource issues.  

* Develop and maintain strong working relationships with stakeholders.
* Responsible for ensuring the resolution of moderate project issues, including the negotiation of necessary changes and


appropriate course of action.  

* Responsible for the input of projects and program items into C55 through project stages.
* May manage/administer small to medium contracts which include selecting and negotiating with vendors/suppliers and


negotiating the terms/conditions of contracts.


***Qualifications:***


* Graduate in Engineering from a University of recognized standing, with a minimum of six years' related experience including


two years' experience in project management.
* Professional member in good standing with Engineers Geoscientists Manitoba (or willingness and ability to attain within a


specified amount of time).
* Demonstrated application of Project Management theory including scoping, estimating, scheduling, procurement, cost control,


risk management and change control in an engineering or construction environment.
* Strong computer skills including experience with scheduling software and the Microsoft Office suite of software. Experience in


the use of SAP and P6 to oversee capital budgets and schedules would be considered an asset.
* Experience related to projects associated with telecommunications, transmission, or distribution, as it relates to design,


construction and commissioning activities would be considered an asset.

MANITOBA HYDRO IS COMMITTED TO DIVERSITY AND EMPLOYMENT EQUITY


 Reference Code: CO57182565\-01


* Demonstrated high degree of initiative, and ability to maintain working relationships and interact effectively with others at all


levels, both inside and outside the corporation.
* Additional formal project management training/education and/or PMP or other similar certification would be considered an


asset.
* Ability to lead, direct and motivate personnel. Proven organizational and leadership abilities.
* Possess a valid Province of Manitoba Driver's Licence.
* Must obtain and maintain a current Personnel Risk Assessment and a "Clear" security rating in accordance with Manitoba



Hydro policy P513\.  

* Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) Training is required and must be completed prior to transfer date and renewed annually.

***Salary Range***



Starting salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The range for the classification is $48\.74\-$67\.29 Hourly,  

$93,398\.24\-$128,947\.78 Annually.


***Apply Now!***



Ready to join a team that energizes Manitoba and puts safety, innovation, and inclusion at the heart of everything we do? Visit  

www.hydro.mb.ca/careers to learn more about this position and to apply online.


**Application deadline: APRIL 10, 2026\.**



We appreciate your interest in Manitoba Hydro and thank all applicants. Only those selected for the next stage of the selection  

process will be contacted.


***If you require accommodations during the recruitment process or need this posting in an accessible format, please let us***  

***know \- we're committed to a barrier\-free experience for all candidates.***
